conclusion.interpretation: JSTprove/Remainder can prove tiny projection, residual-add, and normalization-shaped fixtures, but the checked Softmax, ReLU, and literal MatMul variants expose separate backend/operator blockers. The ReLU blocker is magnitude-sensitive in this probe: scaled variants clear.

fixture_catalog.primary_observation: baseline tiny linear projection proves and verifies
extra Add layer proves and verifies
LayerNormalization-style tiny shape proves and verifies
normalization-like tiny shape proves and verifies
Relu witness hits Remainder range-check capacity
Softmax witness is generated but proof construction refuses an unconstrained op
MatMul compiles but witness generation reports unsupported MatMul

non_claims: not evidence that larger shapes remain small
not evidence that unsupported shapes are impossible in future JSTprove versions
question: Which tiny transformer-adjacent ONNX shapes does JSTprove/Remainder prove today?
